name: facebook-event-scraper
strengths: Focused single-source extraction for event pages and hosted event lists
weaknesses: Facebook only and no broader quality or truth layer
learning_for_eventbaer: Single-source connectors can be valuable as modular adapters but should never be the system core.
possible_implication: Build per-platform connectors as optional source families behind EventBär audit and quality gates.
column41: Extracts detailed Meetup search-result event data with scroll handling
column42: Very narrow scope and no broader product or governance layer
column44: Search-result scrapers can be useful tactical adapters but are weak as long-term system foundations.
column45: Use only as inspiration for connector patterns and search-result handling.
column66: Real local multi-source aggregator with website JSON and iCal outputs
column67: City-bounded and event-only with less visible governance depth
column69: A local aggregator becomes much stronger when one pipeline feeds multiple surfaces.
column70: EventBär should think early in API and feed surfaces not just masterlists.
column91: Strong distribution surface for importing events into WordPress from many sources
column92: Plugin-centric and not a full truth or governance system
column94: Distribution can be a separate product layer on top of ingestion rather than part of the worker core.
column95: A WordPress plugin could become a major later EventBär surface.
column116: Strong event listing UX with tags feeds event pages and calendar outputs
column117: Not a source-audit or truth-engine system
column119: A good event frontend can sit on top of a simpler backend if the user surface is strong.
column120: Keep EventBär data-core and discovery-surface conceptually separable.
column141: Repeatable local scraping and standardization across many public sources
column142: Government-meeting niche is narrower than EventBär consumer events
column144: There is real value in reusable local-source infrastructure not just one-off scrapers.
column145: Treat EventBär source systems as reusable families not isolated hacks.
column166: Collection of source-specific scrapers feeding a central platform via API client
column167: Research and training niche with mixed content model
column169: Many narrow scrapers can still work if the handoff into a common platform is disciplined.
column170: EventBär can support narrow source adapters if the schema handoff is strong.
column191: Good targeted extraction of nightlife event data including prices and lineups
column192: Single-platform with no broader governance or product layer
column194: Subculture-specific connectors can deepen niche coverage where local depth matters.
column195: EventBär can later add niche connectors for nightlife or similar subdomains.
column216: Large standardized place-scraping ecosystem with many spiders and a common format
column217: Not event-native and weak on event time semantics
column219: Location data can be industrialized as its own layer separate from event extraction.
column220: Keep locations and gastro as first-class pipelines not just side effects of event scraping.
column241: Multiple surfaces CLI Web UI and REST API plus broad business-data extraction
column242: Not event-native and highly dependent on one external platform
column244: A strong enrichment tool can be useful even when it is not the truth core.
